seed_problem: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.
mutation: input: skin the max-weight-independent-set selection as issuing rush-hour busker performance permits across a large subway system and place the conflict relation on a general (non-bipartite, possibly dense, up to 5000 pitches / 60000 conflicts) crowd-interference graph with per-pitch audience values, so the easy bipartite/interval special cases vanish and constructing the optimum conflict-free permit set is NP-hard.
title: Metro Busker Permit Scheduling
strategies: trivial baseline: grant only the single highest-value pitch (matches checker baseline, ratio ~0.1)
weight-descending greedy: consider pitches from highest to lowest value, granting each whose grant introduces no conflict with the already-granted set
degree-aware greedy: order pitches by value/(conflict-degree+1) so high-value low-interference pitches are taken first, yielding larger conflict-free sets on dense graphs
multi-restart randomized greedy + maximal extension: run many seeded value-weighted random orderings, greedily build a maximal conflict-free set from each, and keep the highest-value schedule found
